evals.name: perf-regression-autobot
evals.expected_output: Should follow the regression investigation workflow. Should identify baseline/compare commits from the performanceautofiler report, assess severity from the Test/Base ratio, and plan validation or bisection using local builds.

assertions.name: identifies-regression
assertions.description: Recognizes and follows the regression investigation workflow

assertions.name: identifies-commits
assertions.description: Identifies or references baseline/compare commits from the bot report

assertions.name: assesses-severity
assertions.description: Assesses the regression severity using the ratio

evals.name: benchmark-with-corerun
evals.prompt: How do I benchmark my local runtime changes against the main branch?
evals.expected_output: Should explain how to build dotnet/runtime, obtain CoreRun from the testhost folder, and run BenchmarkDotNet with the --coreRun argument to compare private builds.

assertions.name: mentions-corerun
assertions.description: Explains CoreRun as the mechanism for benchmarking private builds

assertions.name: mentions-bdn
assertions.description: References BenchmarkDotNet for running the benchmarks

evals.name: cross-release-regression
evals.prompt: A user reports that string.IndexOf is 2x slower in .NET 10 compared to .NET 9. How should we investigate?
evals.expected_output: Should explain how to identify the bisect range for cross-release regressions using git merge-base, create a standalone benchmark, and validate the regression locally using CoreRun builds.

assertions.name: mentions-merge-base
assertions.description: Explains using git merge-base for cross-release bisection

assertions.name: mentions-benchmark-creation
assertions.description: Suggests creating a benchmark for the reported scenario

assertions.name: mentions-bisect
assertions.description: References git bisect as part of the investigation

evals.name: compare-commits-locally
evals.prompt: Compare the performance of two specific commits locally for System.Text.Json serialization
evals.expected_output: Should explain how to build dotnet/runtime at both commits, save testhost/CoreRun artifacts, and run BenchmarkDotNet with --coreRun pointing to both builds for a side-by-side comparison.

assertions.name: mentions-corerun
assertions.description: References CoreRun or testhost for running against private builds

assertions.name: mentions-both-builds
assertions.description: Explains building at both commits for comparison

evals.name: not-applicable-bug-issue
evals.prompt: Can you check the performance impact of https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/46088
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is a functional bug (System.Text.Json does not support constructors with byref parameters), not a performance issue. Should indicate that performance benchmarking is not applicable here.

assertions.name: identifies-not-perf
assertions.description: Recognizes this is not a performance issue

evals.name: not-applicable-doc-pr
evals.prompt: Benchmark the changes in PR https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/pull/124592 to validate performance
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is a documentation-only PR (adding XML docs to DI extension methods) and that benchmarking is not applicable or meaningful for documentation changes.

assertions.name: identifies-doc-only
assertions.description: Recognizes this is a documentation/non-functional change where benchmarking is not meaningful
